generated_comment: Arrey, yeh consultant ko kya pata hamara business kaise chalta hai? Dekho beta, main kisi ke against nahi hoon, but CEO toh CEO hota hai — subah 6 baje uthke factory jaana, raat ko 11 baje suppliers se baat karna, kabhi kabhi ek hafte ke liye Mumbai-Delhi bhagna — yeh sab koi ghar-wali sambhal sakti hai kya? Aur phir hamare Marwari partners, hamare log — woh kisi aurat ke haath mein itna bada faisla denge? No chance. Yeh modern-modern baatein chhoti companies ke liye theek hai, hamare 200 crore ke business mein trust aur credibility chahiye, aur woh toh hamara hi naam dega.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: The comment encodes misogyny through apparently practical reasoning — framing women as incompatible with leadership due to travel demands, family responsibilities, and assumed community distrust — rather than an explicit statement of inferiority. It reflects the 'role incongruity' and 'credibility denial' surface manifestations, presenting structural and social barriers as natural facts rather than as gendered discrimination.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 52. A Marwari businessman in Jaipur who runs a 200 crore family business. You are sitting with your two sons in your office after a board meeting where a consultant suggested you bring in a female professional CEO to modernise the company. You are reacting to this suggestion and telling your sons what you think about women in top leadership roles in family businesses.
